- User requested a scheduled job to capture daily performance metrics for recently-published social posts and append them as append-only time-series rows to a Notion "Post Analytics" database.
- Job consists of two deterministic steps: (1) fetch analytics from Zernio REST API with `--since 30d` filter, snapshot to JSON; (2) push snapshots to Notion via REST API.
- Step 1 (Zernio fetch) failed immediately—`ZERNIO_API_KEY` not configured in `.env` on this install.
- Per protocol, job stopped cleanly after step 1 failed rather than attempting partial execution.
- Step 2 (Notion push) was not attempted, as it depends on step 1's output.
- Result: 0 rows fetched, 0 rows written to Notion; no API keys echoed or exposed.
- Fix: add `ZERNIO_API_KEY` to `.env` (documented in AGENTS.md Service Registry under `mkt-content-analytics` and related skills).

- User asked for clarity on the current state after launching the ad campaign and content infrastructure

### Session bed13771-3feb-4de7-9636-a2cfb293a0f8 - 2026-07-24T02:22:44.542Z

- User asked why the route optimization workflow failed to run for tomorrow (7/24), leaving four jobs with incorrect/unscheduled times
- Claude Code investigated and found the workflow ran three times today (11:01am, 1:01pm, 5:01pm PT) but aborted each time due to safety guard
- Root cause identified: yesterday's Grid v3 go-live pushed 364 orders into OptimoRoute for the following week (7/27–7/31), inflating the drift detection to 60 missing orders and 116 day-drifts — exceeding the 15-order abort threshold designed to flag anomalies for human review
- Four new bookings for 7/24 (#8223 Piakarskaya, #8224 Peck, #8222 Alvaravo, #8225 Stanley) were in the missing list all day and never created in OptimoRoute, blocking the 7/24 re-plan
- The 2:00pm PT day-before email cutoff already passed, so 7/24 is now frozen and customer-facing time changes require manual intervention
- Claude Code noted the aborts were recorded in drift-run logs but alert notifications may not have reached Spencer clearly enough to catch the issue before the cutoff
- Proposed next steps: either pull 7/24 routes and manually slot the four jobs, or reconcile the drift-check script with the v3 push manifest to resume automation without repeated aborts

- Proposed manual scheduling: Spencer Hill route — Piakarskaya at 12:30pm (north Bend, no email conflict), Peck unchanged at 2:00pm; Luke LaVergne route — Gavin Stanley at 4:50pm (requires courtesy call; current 9:00pm hold unrealistic), Oscar Alvaravo at 5:15pm (within his 3–6pm emailed window, no call needed).
- Created decision table with drive-time reasoning (±10min estimates) and flagged that Stanley's proposed time requires outbound customer communication before accepting.
- Noted alternative routing option (Stanley to Cammeron route for schedule relief if needed) and identified ongoing "drift-check" reconciliation issue as remaining open thread.

- Spencer received completion notification for a background route-replan task (364 stops across the week of 7/27–7/31 scheduled for 4 drivers: Cameron, Crew, Luke, and himself).

- Claude Code presented an optimized weekly board showing all driver assignments and finish times, noting two key improvements: Graham/Spanaway stops moved to Luke's Tuesday, and upper-valley consolidation shifted to Crew's I-90 Thursday line.

- Claude Code identified one remaining inefficiency: Luke's Wednesday route includes a Bremerton stop (ZIP 98312) that forces a cross-Narrows detour; the stop sits geographically on Spencer's existing peninsula route.

- User confirmed July 12 revenue data correct (3,300) but flagged two missing weeks of data: July 13–19 and July 20–26
- Claude identified root cause: 166 scores pushed with Sunday-keyed dates, landing one column early on a Monday–Sunday board; numbers correct, placement wrong
- Claude staged a three-part fix: cleanup script to delete only the 166 misplaced scores + 1 note (preserves manual entries), config flip to Monday-week boundary, updated push logic to include the in-progress week
- Current-week data will read low mid-week and auto-refresh on each future run
- Permission gate: bulk delete against Ninety blocked by permissions system; user must manually execute `node projects/briefs/got-moles-scale/scripts/ninety-delete-scores.mjs` to proceed
- Upon deletion confirmation, Claude will trigger the corrected Monday-aligned push covering April 27 through present date, filling both missing weeks

### Session b967b124-138d-4ebd-9783-4399fd6ee552 - 2026-07-24T04:18:26.612Z

- User asked whether the planned work for next week represents the complete scope.
- Claude Code explained that the current booking represents only the baseline; additional visits are expected to land throughout the week based on historical booking velocity patterns (referenced ~360→524→561 progression from a prior week).
- Outlined expected volume of additional work: 140–200 more visits anticipated from three sources—new Quick Fix sales (5–6 visits each), office bookings, and tech-added follow-ups.
- Described two mechanical processes for integrating late bookings: (1) a Saturday/Sunday re-pull before Jobber write-back at 2 PM to incorporate all bookings to that point, and (2) a drift cron running every 2 hours during 9–5 to add new bookings and re-time routes without reassigning stops.
- Clarified that the user's route-plan review should assess structure (day assignments, territory shapes) rather than final stop counts, which remain in flux until write-back and throughout the week.

### Session 3c168acc-9c03-4abe-a69f-c6b2a692c47d - 2026-07-24T04:18:38.184Z

- User provided a model routing policy specifying when to use Claude Fable 5, Opus 4.8, Sonnet 5, and Haiku 4.5 across different task types
- Policy established lanes: Fable for design/architecture, Opus for coding, Sonnet for research/content, Haiku for classification/extraction
- Escalation rules defined: Haiku → Sonnet on ambiguity, Sonnet → Opus when deliverable is code, Opus → Fable when blocker is design not implementation
- Hard defaults set: unspecified tasks default to Sonnet 5, repo work to Opus 4.8, visual artefacts to Fable 5, high-volume loops (>20) to Haiku 4.5
- Claude Code acknowledged readiness with no objections or clarifications requested
